notes: The skill provides the most value on the CSV export eval (+43% pass rate) — it adds date boundary defaults, row caps, and audit logging that the baseline completely misses.
Password change eval shows +25% improvement — the skill prompts explicit input validation at the handler boundary rather than relying on service-layer validation.

File upload eval shows identical scores (87.5%) for both configurations — the tenant-scoping assertion fails in both cases. The skill mentions 'verify parent ownership before creating children' but neither implementation actually queries the entity to verify org membership.

The 'tenant scoping' assertion for file uploads may be too strict — the existing codebase's upload handler also doesn't verify entity ownership. This could be a gap in the skill's guidance rather than a failure to follow it.
Auth extractor and Content-Disposition assertions pass 100% in both configurations — these are non-discriminating (the model knows to do this regardless of skill).
